title: Veronicastrum cerasifolium
taxonomy: Veronicastrum cerasifolium is a species of flowering plant in the family Veronicaceae. It is a perennial plant native to China and the Himalayas. This species is often classified as a member of the balm family. Further classification includes its order, Lamiales. Veronicastrum cerasifolium is a member of the Veronicaceae family within the clade Asterids.

The plant's leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. intro: Veronicastrum cerasifolium, also known as Red Veronica or Red Foxglove, is a flowering plant species within the Veronica family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Veronicastrum cerasifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Veronicastrum cerasifolium include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to support cardiovascular health and reduce oxidative stress.
